Standout_idea: Provenance-in-frontmatter: each skill's description names the source framework AND the trigger ('Based on Teresa Torres' Continuous Discovery Habits. Use when…'), so auto-invocation matches intent while the body faithfully encodes the named method (OST levels, V/U/V/F risks, literal RICE/ICE formulas).

Strict noun/verb split enforced by tooling: skills = auto-loaded knowledge (no $ARGUMENTS), commands = user-invoked chains (single $ARGUMENTS), commands may only reference same-plugin skills — and validate_plugins.py is a CI gate that enforces every part of this contract (name=dir, semver, trigger phrasing, word-count bounds, cross-ref scoping, README sections).

Document-intent-first AI-shipping pipeline: /ship-check generates a fixed documentation set (architecture/flows/permissions/secrets/test-coverage) as the spec, THEN security-audit-static and derive-tests audit the real code against that documented intent — turning generated docs into the source of truth for review.

Self-refuting audit prompt: security-audit-static instructs 'self-refute every finding… report only evidence-backed risks', baking an adversarial anti-hallucination check into a single stateless skill instead of a separate critic agent.

Deliberately stateless by design — AGENTS.md is a redirect to CLAUDE.md to avoid duplicated guidance; no memory store, no subagents, no persisted artifacts — orchestration is pure prompt-level skill chaining inside one conversation.

Adaptive Decision Ladder: interactive 'advisor' skills script a named-dimension question ladder (Q1 Stage / Q2 Team / Q3 Need / Q4 Data), each with 3-4 numbered options carrying an inline rationale gloss and an 'Or describe (custom)' escape hatch, then synthesize a recommended framework + alternative-with-tradeoffs + reassessment triggers — a reusable shape for any 'help me choose X' decision skill.

workshop-facilitation as a shared interaction-protocol skill: the elicitation UX (entry modes Guided/Context-dump/Best-guess, one-question-per-turn, progress labels like 'Context Q3/8', multi-select parsing, 'recommendations only at decision points', closing 'Assumptions to Validate') is factored out once and referenced by every advisor via a relative SKILL.md link, with a documented conflict rule — meta-DRY across skills.

Commands declare their skill chain in frontmatter: a `uses:` array + `outputs:` list in commands/<name>.md turns a slash-command into a self-documenting workflow router over named skills, with checkpoint gates and explicit 'Next Steps' routing to sibling commands.

Pedagogy-as-constraint: CLAUDE.md makes teaching load-bearing ('Never strip explanation for brevity — coaching is the work') and enforces a 7-tick Quality Checklist + a fixed 6-section anatomy, so every skill doubles as a human-learning artifact and an agent-executable spec.

Frontmatter-driven catalog generation: scripts/generate-catalog.py turns per-skill YAML into machine-readable indexes + human views, giving a vector-DB-free 'map of where skills live' that stays in sync via a build step.

The SKILL+TEMPLATE+EXAMPLE triad makes 'quality' a file, not a prompt: the skill defines method, the template defines structure, and a worked EXAMPLE.md per skill anchors the quality bar so outputs calibrate to a professional example rather than the model's average.

Trigger accuracy is empirically tested, not assumed: every skill ships an evals/trigger-fixtures.json of ≥16 labeled queries with deliberate near-miss negatives aimed at 'collision-partner' skills, and run-router-evals.mjs measures whether the description-driven router actually fires the right skill — auto-invocation as a tested contract.

A SessionStart phase-router hook reads the git branch and working-dir artifacts to infer the Triple-Diamond phase, then injects the top-4 phase-relevant skills into context with one line — context-aware skill recommendation with zero user input, failing silent.

pm-critic is a true fresh-context adversary: read-only, single-turn, maps each artifact type to its originating skill's SKILL.md contract, grades P0–P3, mandates a concrete fix per finding, and can refuse with a P0 when its inputs are incomplete — separation of critique from authorship.

The orchestrator's PRODUCED/EMPTY/FAILED result taxonomy drives governed advancement: GUARDED-AUTO advances only on substantive artifacts, forces a checkpoint on empty/ambiguous steps, and halts unconditionally on failure — a go/no-go state machine over a skill chain.

A multi-platform validator-parity check keeps bash, PowerShell, and CI validator inventories synchronized, so the same contracts (counts, cross-refs, placeholders, fabricated metrics, quote sourcing) are enforced identically across harnesses.

Skill-first, not command-first: despite the README advertising '7 slash commands', the commands/ directory contains exactly ONE file (brainstorm.md) — all 8 skills are invoked by description-match auto-invocation. The single command exists only to front the interactive brainstorming skill, which benefits from an explicit entry point; document-generation skills are left to trigger themselves on intent.

Tool-agnostic ~~category placeholders + a CONNECTORS.md resolver: skill prose never hardcodes a vendor ('pull from ~~tracker', 'post via ~~chat'); the same SKILL.md works whether the user connected Linear or Jira, Slack or Teams. CONNECTORS.md is the single index that maps placeholder->actual MCP server, and skills are told to consult it for unknown placeholders — clean separation of workflow logic from integration wiring.

Remote HTTP-MCP connector layer in .mcp.json: 16 OAuth'd type:http endpoints (Linear/Atlassian/Notion/Figma/Amplitude/Pendo/Intercom/Fireflies/SimilarWeb/Slack) wired declaratively per plugin, including a region-split amplitude-eu and empty-url placeholder entries for Gmail/Calendar — the integration surface is config, not code.

Methodology-dense SKILL.md bodies with enforced output contracts and named anti-hallucination rules (write-spec's P0 discipline, synthesize-research's quote-attribution-by-type and confidence-level rules, metrics-review's mandatory current/comparison/trend triple) — the PM judgment is encoded as hard rules inside a flat single-file skill, no bundled references/templates/examples subfolders.

Audience x type routing inside a single skill (stakeholder-update): a 4x5 matrix of update-type by audience each with its own depth budget and template, plus RAG + ROAM risk frameworks, all in one SKILL.md rather than separate skills per audience.

Per-skill dual manifest: every skill ships a Claude-style SKILL.md AND an agents/openai.yaml sidecar (version:1, invocation.trigger: implicit|explicit), making one skill library genuinely portable across Claude Code, Codex, Pi and OpenClaw from a single source.

Three independent eval families as plain Python: behavioral skill-output (token-overlap vs fixtures/live endpoint), routing accuracy (keyword-rule 'did it pick the right skill'), and A/B memory-impact (answer quality with vs without memory search) — skill selection and memory itself are treated as testable.

validate_skills.py as a real skill-quality contract: enforces folder==name, required frontmatter, mandatory process + When-to-Use/When-Not-to-Use sections, the openai.yaml sidecar, AND greps the whole repo for stale path references — so structural quality can't silently rot.

Session-eval → AGENTS.md self-correction loop with a fixed template (judgement enum + axial-code checkboxes like over-engineering/missed-requirements + an explicit 'AGENTS.md Improvements' field) reviewed weekly and folded back into the governing instruction file.

ost-intake as a dedicated normalization/handoff stage that reframes solution-flavored outcomes into measurable ones and feature-nodes into moments-in-time, emitting four clean fields as the baton the OST synthesis skill executes against.

GOALS.md as an enforced relevance gate, not a passive doc: every task must cite a goal or the agent halts, and P0/P1 are hard-capped (3/7) so prioritization is structurally bounded rather than advisory.

An MCP server that makes 'don't create duplicate or vague tasks' a deterministic tool: SequenceMatcher title*0.7 + keyword-overlap*0.3 dedup plus a vague-verb regex that forces a clarification question before any task is written.

Session Evals as a self-improvement loop for the operator's own AI use: trace_to_eval.py parses ~/.claude/projects/*.jsonl sessions into reviewable .md eval files with user-intent, collapsed turn flow, tool-usage table, an auto-suggested judgement (success/partial/failure/pending), axial codes (good-context-gathering, efficient-tool-use, iterative-refinement, task-tracking, incomplete), and an empty human-annotation block.
Integration-as-skill triad (README + SETUP_SKILL.md + mcp-config.json) so adding a tool like Granola is 'tell Claude to set up the integration' and the agent self-installs from the template.
File-as-memory layered stack (AGENTS.md → GOALS.md → Tasks/ → Knowledge/) read every session, with per-task Progress Logs as the continuity journal — persistence with zero vector DB.

Single-skill multi-domain operator: instead of a fleet of skills/agents, ONE SKILL.md dispatches across 7 domains + 40+ frameworks through an intent→framework→knowledge-file routing table — the routing table doubles as the index of where each framework lives (name-keyed retrieval, no vector DB).

Two-tier quality gates: universal gates in the root file apply always; domain-specific gates ship INSIDE each knowledge module and only enter context when that module is lazy-loaded — context-scoped enforcement that scales with what's loaded.

STATUS completion contract (DONE / DONE_WITH_CONCERNS / BLOCKED / NEEDS_CONTEXT) on a prose PM skill, plus a 3-strikes-then-BLOCKED guard — a machine-readable success/failure signal for inherently fuzzy deliverables.

Per-module Coaching-Mode interaction tables (Trigger / Action / Stop-When) turn 'be a tough peer' into deterministic, finishable challenge loops with explicit exit conditions (e.g. stop pushing after 2 rounds, or when the user segments the churn data).

Always-on Framing gate separated from opt-in Coaching: quality-control pushback (solution smuggling, no metrics, scope mixing) fires once on every artifact request, while interrogation-style coaching only fires on explicit consent — a clean separation of 'never ship garbage' from 'don't nag.'

Radical minimalism: each skill is ~20-40 lines of Markdown — 2-field frontmatter (name+description only) + Purpose + Steps + an Output section of empty headings — proving a usable, auto-invocable PM skill needs almost no scaffolding.

The output contract IS the skill: behavior is steered almost entirely by a fixed set of required Markdown subheadings plus a constrained verdict enum (Worth Solving / Build Now-Later-Do Not Build / Ready-At Risk-Not Ready), forcing every run to terminate in a discrete decision.

One shared fictional product ('AI Meeting Notes') threads through every skill's example.md, so the 15 worked examples read as a coherent end-to-end case study (problem -> JTBD -> assumptions -> prioritization -> PRD critique -> launch readiness) rather than 15 unrelated samples.
Description field doubles as the router: with no trigger/tools fields, the 'Use when ...' clause in description is the sole auto-invocation signal, a clean demonstration of description-as-dispatcher.

Radically minimal frontmatter as a deliberate design choice: ONLY `name` + `description`, with all trigger logic ('Use when asked about X, Y, Z') written into the description prose so auto-invocation is pure semantic match — no custom trigger DSL, maximally portable.
Trigger phrases embedded directly in the description as natural-language 'Use when...' clauses, making each skill self-documenting about its own activation conditions.

Method-as-imperative-rules: frameworks are compiled into terse ALWAYS/NEVER/CRITICAL directives with a concrete counter-example attached (e.g. 'the job is share a polished doc with investors, not export to PDF'), which reads more like a checklist than an essay.

Output-contract discipline baked into individual skills (RICE: show all math, confidence<100% without data; PRD: mandatory counter-metrics and Out-of-Scope section) — lightweight gap/anti-hallucination enforcement without any external validator.

Citable-framework requirement as a hard authoring rule: every skill must name its source methodology (Intercom RICE, Linear Method, Mom Test, Christensen/Klement Forces of Progress, Dunford positioning, Torres OST, Basecamp Shape Up), giving the whole pack provenance.

One shared `startup-context` foundation file (8 fixed sections) that EVERY skill is contractually required to `reads:` first — a single-source-of-truth context anchor that converts a generic skill library into a company-tailored one without any vector DB.

Declarative `reads:` + `related:` frontmatter forms a static skill graph: `reads` points every skill at the context file, `related` wires the workflow chain (prd-writing → roadmap-planning → mvp-scoping) — a map-file approach to chaining with zero runtime retrieval machinery.

prd-writing's lightweight(2-3pg)-vs-full(5-8pg) branch as step 1 — an explicit scope gate that right-sizes the artifact to company stage before any drafting, plus assumption-tracking with evidence + invalidation criteria baked into the workflow.

A CONTRIBUTING.md that codifies a strict 7-section body + 4-field frontmatter contract for all 50 skills, making the library uniform and forkable — an authoring constitution rather than ad-hoc skills.

Header-based SKILL.md contract (Trigger/Context/Instructions/Constraints/Output Format/Examples/Anti-patterns) with a shipped templates/SKILL-TEMPLATE.md as the reusable skeleton — a deliberately minimal, no-frontmatter, no-bundled-resources skill format optimized for copy-paste adoption.
Contrastive 'Good vs Bad' worked examples on the SAME input, each annotated with the failure reason, teaching the model the discrimination boundary rather than just the target output.

Scoring GUIDES with numeric thresholds baked into each rubric dimension (Strong/Moderate/Weak defined by concrete economics like '<1,000 customers to $1M ARR'), turning subjective PM judgment into a repeatable scorecard + GO/ITERATE/STOP verdict.

README-as-architecture-doc: the 'CLAUDE.md = identity / skills = tasks' split is the repo's actual product, taught with a token-economy rationale (always-loaded vs on-demand) and a from-scratch skill-authoring tutorial.
Mandatory interactive 'Step 1' elicitation gate with coded pushback rules (refuse vague 'everyone' answers) before any generation.

Filesystem-as-context-firewall: context-scanner explicitly returns NO JSON to callers — it writes validated, provenance-stamped facts to .claude/product-context/* and the invoking agent re-reads the files after control returns, keeping the scan's raw output out of the caller's window.

Model-tiered fleet by frontmatter: Haiku for the file-grinding context-scanner, Sonnet for routing/doc-gen specialists, Opus for the adversarial idea-skeptic — cost/capability matched per role in one line of YAML.

A debate-protocol encoded as a reusable SKILL (team-coordination): named challenge formats, steel-man-before-attack rule, weighted synthesis percentages, and five named anti-patterns (Consensus Theater, False Precision...) make multi-agent disagreement a first-class, auditable mechanism rather than vibes.
PRD template auto-selection via a transparent 3-axis 0-10 complexity score that reads the product-context files to inform scoring and shows the user its reasoning before generating.

Progressive context scaffolding: pm-setup writes only the 2 essential files up front and delegates creation of the other 6 to journey-stage agents, so the context tree grows as the product matures instead of demanding a 20-question intake.

Config-not-manual minimalism as a stated design principle: a sub-60-line CLAUDE.md with a 9-field fill-in form, an explicit 'every rule must earn its place / prune at ~150 lines' anti-bloat gate, and the claim that short instructions outperform long ones — context engineering by subtraction.

Reviewer personas as a 6-row markdown table + a verbal 'review as [role]' contract and a prescribed sequential chain, delivering 80% of a critic-agent panel with zero agent infrastructure (no agents/ dir, no tool scoping) — a cheap, copyable pattern for non-engineer PMs.

PRD-writer as a 6-stage lifecycle state machine where the SAME doc evolves (speclet -> full PRD -> post-launch results link that 'closes the build-test-learn loop'), with stage-specific verification checklists — spec-as-source-of-truth done as delta edits.

Pervasive Good-vs-Bad few-shot grounding: every skill carries side-by-side vague-vs-specific example pairs, and prd-writer adds an 'Anti-Patterns (From Analyzing 500+ PRDs)' catalogue — teaching the model the failure mode, not just the target.

Mandatory anti-metric rule in metrics-definer ('if you cannot identify one, you have not thought hard enough about perverse incentives') — encoding adversarial measurement thinking as a non-skippable output slot.

Self-improvement protocol that routes corrections: trivial -> propose CLAUDE.md rule (with approval), recurring -> propose a scoped .claude/rules/ file 'instead of bloating this file' — a capture loop with a built-in placement/altitude decision.

Agents-as-skill-orchestrators: the 3 .md agents contain almost no domain logic — they are pure pipelines that invoke the 20 atomic /slash skills in a fixed order with approval gates between phases, so the same skill is reusable both standalone (user types /create-prd) and as a pipeline step. Clean separation of orchestration from capability.

The whole Opportunity Solution Tree is realized as a filesystem-as-state-machine: snapshots/ → synthesis/ → opportunities/ → solutions/ → assumptions/ folders ARE the discovery state, and each skill reads the predecessor folder and writes the successor — no database, the directory tree is the memory and the index.

Anti-sycophancy is encoded as a first-class always-on rule: the root .mdc bans specific hedging phrases ('interesting', 'you might consider'), names PM failure patterns to flag (solution-seeking-problem, hypothetical users, interest/demand conflation), and demands named personas over categories — a reusable 'sparring partner' governance layer.

ICE scoring as a fully declarative skill with calibrated evidence-weighting tables (self-belief 0.01× up to market data 3.0×, with caps) and a hard 'score only on stated evidence' rule — sophisticated quantitative prioritization with zero executable code, just prompt-encoded rubrics.

Approval-gated, resumable pipelines: every agent step offers Proceed/Revise/Go-Deeper/Go-Back and can resume from any phase using already-saved artifacts, making long PM workflows interruptible and idempotent across sessions.

Each agent ships method + structure together: the PM file embeds five copy-paste deliverable templates (PRD, Opportunity Assessment w/ RICE table, Now/Next/Later roadmap with a mandatory 'What We're NOT Building and why' table, GTM brief, Sprint Health snapshot) as fenced blocks, so the agent is a self-contained PM toolkit, not just a persona.

One canonical agent source compiled to 12+ tool formats by convert.sh, including header-keyword-driven file SPLITS (OpenClaw SOUL.md/AGENTS.md/IDENTITY.md) and format-specific frontmatter injection (Antigravity SKILL.md gets risk/source/date_added; Codex gets TOML-escaped instructions; Kimi gets split yaml+system.md).

The nexus 8-agent parallel discovery deliberately ENGINEERS for both convergence and divergence: a synthesis pass reports the 4 conclusions all agents reached independently as strong signals AND preserves 4 unresolved cross-domain TENSIONS for human judgment rather than forcing consensus.

Receiver-addressed memory tagging: a deliverable is tagged with the DOWNSTREAM agent's name ('frontend-developer') so the next agent auto-discovers its inputs on recall by project name—turning a copy-paste handoff chain into autonomous tag-routed retrieval over an MCP memory server with remember/recall/rollback/search.

Hardwired filesystem memory contract: the Senior PM reads its spec from a fixed `ai/memory-bank/site-setup.md` path with a 125-char max-quote rule and a no-scope-creep gate, and writes 30-60min tasks to `ai/memory-bank/tasks/[slug]-tasklist.md`—a concrete spec-as-source-of-truth + doc-handoff convention.

The `description` frontmatter field is engineered as the auto-invocation classifier: each agent embeds 3-4 structured <example> blocks (Context / user / assistant / <commentary>) that act as few-shot routing exemplars — the repo's whole 'orchestration' is just Claude Code matching a request against these example-rich descriptions, no router code at all.

A near-identical RIGID system-prompt skeleton is reused across all ~39 agents (identity → 6 numbered '**primary responsibilities**' each with 6 sub-bullets → named framework blocks → Anti-Patterns → fenced Templates → Metrics), making the prompts feel authored by one template — a replicable house style for at-scale agent authoring.
PM methodology is encoded as a literal bulleted MENU inside the prompt (RICE/Kano/JTBD/OST/OKR in sprint-prioritizer) rather than as runnable code or a separate reference file — frameworks-as-prose.

trend-researcher hard-codes NUMERIC decision gates in natural language (trend 1-4wk momentum = build it; >50% WoW hashtag growth = high potential; min 100K users), turning a vibe judgment into a quasi-deterministic rubric.

The 6-day/6-week sprint metaphor is the unifying context substitute: with no CLAUDE.md or shared context file, every agent re-states the cadence in its own prompt (sprint-prioritizer W1-6, studio-producer W0-6), so 'studio culture' is distributed redundantly across agents instead of centralized.

studio-coach is a pure-prompt orchestrator distinguished ONLY by being granted the `Task` tool plus a 'PROACTIVELY' description — least-privilege tool allowlists are the actual capability/role boundary between agents.

The entire multi-role product team is pure declarative config: role/goal/backstory in agents.yaml + description/expected_output/context/agent in tasks.yaml, with crew.py being nearly boilerplate — the 'team' is data, not code, so adding/reordering roles is a YAML edit.

tasks.yaml `context:` lists make dependencies explicit and form a fan-in: the final PRD task pulls all five upstream artifacts (purpose, backlog, QA, features, design) into one synthesis step — a clean doc-handoff DAG expressed in config.

Every expected_output repeats the meta-constraint 'written in a way that is optimal for an agentic coder to create the product', and the final task is bound to obey knowledge/rules.txt — the PRD is purpose-built as downstream agent context, not human reading.

knowledge/rules.txt functions as a portable 'constitution for writing agentic-coder specs' (design-before-code, start small, TDD, ask humans, keep docs current) injected as crew-level knowledge so all agents share the same governing rules.

bmad-spec's append-only .memlog.md with a re-derived SPEC.md: the kernel doc is never hand-edited but regenerated from a chronological log on every run, so any number of upstream skills can update the same spec in any order without merge drift while stable capability IDs survive — a CM-21 self-rederiving-doc pattern that solves multi-writer context collision.

The story file as a self-contained context-handoff baton: bmad-create-story SELECTIVE_LOADs only the relevant shards of PRD/architecture/epics, distills them into a fat 'Dev Notes + References (with source paths/sections)' block, and emits a status-gated {epic}-{num}-title.md — so a fresh dev agent gets exactly the compacted context it needs, cited, without re-reading the whole planning corpus.

base→team→user 3-layer customize.toml resolution: personas (role/identity/style/principles/persistent_facts/activation hooks) are tunable per org and per user WITHOUT forking the skill, while core identity stays locked — a clean self/team-tuning layer over shared skills.

Persona-council 'party mode' with a resolve_party.py roster script and four escalating execution modes (inline-voiced → auto → subagent → persistent agent-team) plus per-party memory — multi-persona deliberation that scales from cheap inline role-play to real independent subagents.

A deterministic skill-validator (12 rules, JSON output, in CI via npm run quality) that enforces description 'Use when' triggers, step-NN-description.md naming with ## NEXT links, and bans cross-skill file paths in favor of skill:name invocation — making a 34-skill catalog mechanically consistent and safely refactorable.

GitHub Issues used as the agents' shared database: tasks leave local files and become real issues/sub-issues (via gh-sub-issue) so humans and multiple agents read/write the same external state, surviving any single context window.

Five-phase doc-as-baton pipeline (PRD -> epic -> tasks -> issues -> code) where every artifact is a frontmatter'd markdown file in a fixed .claude/ tree and each phase's reference doc reads the previous artifact and writes the next.

A per-issue analysis file (<N>-analysis.md) that pre-computes independent 'streams' with explicit file scope and 'Can Start' flags, turning one issue into a parallel-agent launch plan with least-privilege file ownership.
One epic = one git worktree shared by all its parallel agents, with coordination by 'Issue #N' commits + git status checks and a hard 'never --force' rule, instead of a worktree-per-agent explosion.
Tracking is deliberately LLM-free: 14 bash scripts scan the file tree for status/standup/blocked/next, with track.md explicitly telling the model to just run the script and present output.

Document-as-baton chain: a clean specs/<feature>/ folder where each command writes exactly one artifact the next reads (spec → research → data-model/contracts → plan → tasks → code), making the written spec the executable source of truth rather than throwaway scaffolding.

Declarative `handoffs:` frontmatter with labeled next-step prompts and `send: true` auto-advance — the multi-step workflow is wired into each command's YAML, not buried in prose, so any harness can render it as next-action buttons.

A versioned constitution (semver + auto-generated Sync Impact Report) loaded by every downstream command and treated by /analyze as a non-negotiable CRITICAL gate — governance encoded as durable context, not a one-time prompt.
Checklists reframed as 'unit tests for English' that validate the REQUIREMENTS' clarity/completeness, with /implement hard-gating on their PASS/FAIL counts before writing any code.

A documented decision to DISABLE subagent forking (FORK_CONTEXT_COMMANDS empty) because the would-be context-firewall actually leaked a 500-line report back into main context and froze sessions — a rare real-world negative result on the 'fresh-context subagent' pattern.

Four-layer runtime template resolution (overrides > presets > extensions > core) plus version-pinned bundles, letting an org enforce its own spec/plan formats and role-based provisioning without forking the framework.

Lettered multiple-choice clarifying questions: forcing the AI to phrase its 3–5 PRD questions as numbered items with A/B/C/D options so a non-technical user can answer the entire intake in one terse line ('1A, 2C, 3B') — friction-free elicitation that any PM enablement program could copy verbatim.

The literal 'Go' gate: a hard, human-spoken confirmation token between high-level plan and detailed breakdown — cheap to implement (it's one sentence in a prompt), but it converts a runaway planner into a reviewable two-phase process.

Tasks-file-as-progress-ledger: the same markdown artifact is plan, spec, and live state — the agent edits `- [ ]`→`- [x]` in place after each sub-task, so the file alone reconstructs 'where are we' for any fresh session with zero extra memory machinery.
One-sub-task-then-stop-for-approval loop: the entire 'safety' of the framework is a human checkpoint after every atomic step, not tooling — a maximally tool-agnostic way to keep a human in the loop.

Radical minimalism: an entire opinionated SDLC (spec → plan → checkpointed build) delivered as TWO plain .md files with zero code, frontmatter, or runtime — proof that 'prompt templates referenced by @filename' is a viable cross-tool distribution format.

Deterministic, unfakable completion token: SHIP_CHECK_OK only emits after a HARD exit-code oracle (Gate 5, re-runs operator tests in a network-isolated podman sandbox, fail-closed, 'no override path') AND a reachability sweep proving new code is WIRED/EXEMPT not ORPHAN — completion is structural, not a claim, with exactly one audit-logged [OVERRIDE] escape hatch.

Letter-graded, placeholder-proof PRD as a deterministic gate: validate_prd scores EXCELLENT/GOOD/ACCEPTABLE/NEEDS_WORK and HARD-fails on any bare TBD/TODO/{{...}}, but lets a deferred decision pass IF tagged with an explicit `reason:` — turning 'is this spec actually done' into a non-zero exit code.

Stateless orchestrator that survives context loss: the `go` router holds no state — every phase skill re-reads current_phase() from pipeline.json on entry, so compaction or a fresh session resumes exactly where it left off; the file IS the memory.

Dependency-wave fleet with checker-gated sequential merges: file-disjoint waves of model-agnostic worktree workers report through a durable inbox (4 fixed terminal statuses, never trusted prose), merge one-at-a-time behind a checker gate, and never touch main until a final green PR — with a sole-writer rule on the control plane.

PreToolUse phase-gate hook (gate_enforcer.py) that intercepts advance_phase and returns permissionDecision:'deny' with concrete reasons unless typed per-phase evidence is present (e.g. GENERATE needs grade EXCELLENT/GOOD + ≥1 task + ≥1.0 subtask coverage) — the engine, not the prompt, enforces the gate.

Token-economy cross-vendor routing: Claude/Codex/Gemini are separate quota pools on a capability ladder (Fable→haiku); complexity-2 scaffolding gets cheap models, escalation only on failure, with conservative/balanced/performance economy settings and a local economy-report proving per-model success-rate/latency.

Orchestrator→Executor→Checker triad with model tiering AND a privilege firewall: opus orchestrator plans and fans out, sonnet executors implement, a sonnet checker with READ-ONLY tools verifies against spec and can only flag (never fix), so failures loop back — a clean separation of plan/do/verify that mirrors a real dev team and structurally prevents self-grading.

The Task object itself is the inter-agent context packet: a single typed record carries details, testStrategy, dependencies, acceptanceCriteria, scopeBoundaries, relevantFiles, codebasePatterns and an operation-surviving user `metadata` block — so the orchestrator hands an executor just an id and the full execution context travels with the store, not the chat.

One tag-partitioned tasks.json behind a pluggable IStorage adapter (FormatHandler/FileOperations/PathResolver) that transparently swaps a local file for a Supabase API store via a 'file'|'api'|'auto' StorageType — the same agents work identically against local or cloud state.

Complexity analysis is wired into expansion: analyze-complexity scores tasks 1-10 and emits a recommendedSubtasks count plus a pre-baked expansionPrompt, which expand-task then consumes to break the task down using a work-type template (feature/bug/refactor) — analysis output directly parameterizes the next generation step.

A command-chaining DSL (command-pipeline) with state variables, conditionals, loops, parallel branches and join — letting a whole PM workflow (parse-prd → expand-all → filter:complex>70 → assign:senior → sprint-plan) be expressed as one composable spec.

File-based Message Bus: every agent owns a `.apm/bus/<slug>/` dir with single-purpose task.md/report.md/handoff.md channel files, and a 'read-before-write / clear-before-compose' protocol turns the filesystem into a typed, durable inter-agent message queue that survives context resets.

Project state lives entirely OUTSIDE agent context in a scaffolded `.apm/` tree (spec/plan/tracker + indexed per-stage memory logs), so any agent is a stateless executor that reconstructs from files — the core enabler of cheap handoffs.

Handoff deliberately splits PAST (Handoff Log: coordination history, VC state, learned user prefs, instance numbers) from PRESENT (Handoff Prompt: outstanding tasks + reconstruction steps), so a fresh instance rebuilds working knowledge procedurally rather than trusting one memory blob.

Self-contained Task Prompts with an enforced frontmatter contract (stage/task numbers, pre-constructed log_path, dependency flag) and a same-agent vs cross-agent dependency rule that decides how much context to inline — workers are intentionally scoped to only their prompt.

Archive Explorer subagent reads whole prior-session archives but returns only a 5-category synthesis plus file-path verification handles — a context firewall that also forces the Planner to spot-check for stale context against the live codebase.

Fresh-context adversarial validator: research-validator is spawned with ONLY the research refs and zero project memory, explicitly so it cannot rationalize bad sources — a structural (not prompted) defense against confirmation bias, with a 3-iteration re-research loop and graceful partially_validated fallback.

Meta-skill (skills-generator): an agent that writes 7 project-specific enforcement SKILL.md files into the target repo, each stamped with prd_version, citing real RF-/DR-/ADR- IDs, with a 'zero curly-brace tokens in output' contract — the plugin's durable value is the enforcement scaffolding it leaves behind, not the one-shot PRD.

Versioned skills as a drift gate: every generated skill's prd_version is checked against the PRD header by prd-guardian, which warns 'skills are stale, run /prd-evolve' — turning documentation drift into a detectable, blockable condition.

Delta-aware /prd-evolve: requirements-analyst delta mode returns affected_artifacts, and ONLY those agents/sections are re-run in parallel; untouched artifacts are preserved and reported, with skills appending Evolution Log rows rather than being regenerated.

Local-first docs cache enforced three ways: a generated PreToolUse hook (stderr reminder on WebFetch/WebSearch), a project-docs-stack skill with a 'never search twice for the same topic' Hard Rule, and a docs/stack/README.md index table — a no-vector-DB persistent doc memory across sessions.

Source-discipline via blocked_domains: official-researcher bans ~25 forum/blog/social domains on every search and grades each source is_official, so the PRD is built only on vendor docs / standards bodies / gov portals.

Verification is a 7-step Unified Pipeline that escalates cost by claim complexity: free zero-LLM graph checks (cycles/contradictions/PageRank) first, then KS-adaptive multi-judge consensus with statistical early-stopping, then NLI entailment, then multi-agent debate only for CRITICAL high-variance claims — verification effort is budgeted, not uniform.
An honest verdict taxonomy (PASS / SPEC-COMPLETE / NEEDS-RUNTIME / INCONCLUSIVE / FAIL) with an explicit rule banning universal-PASS, fighting LLM confirmation bias at the output-contract level.

64 numbered, machine-checkable output rules (story-point arithmetic must balance, no self-referencing dependencies, no TODO test bodies, parameterized queries only) enforced by a mandatory pre-delivery self-check that corrects violations before writing files.

A per-PRD-type context table (8 types × sections/clarification-Qs/RAG-hops/strategy-focus) that deterministically scales depth — a proposal gets 1 RAG hop and 5-6 questions; an incident gets 4 hops and 10-12 questions — exposed at runtime via an MCP get_prd_context_info tool.

Strategy effectiveness tracking (Algorithm 17): logs each thinking strategy's actual vs expected confidence gain and flags strategies underperforming on <5% of assignments — a self-measuring reasoning library.

The workflow is DATA, not code: a single schema.yaml declares artifacts + requires[] dependencies + per-artifact instruction prompts, and a TypeScript artifact-graph DAG (graph/resolver/state) turns it into an enforced propose->specs/design->tasks->apply state machine.

CLI-as-instruction-server: slash commands are thin shims that make the agent shell out to `openspec instructions <id> --json` and `openspec status --json` at each step, pulling ONLY the current artifact's rules + next pending step into context (true just-in-time disclosure instead of a giant system prompt).

Completion is inferred from disk: state.ts detectCompleted() decides which stages are done purely by checking whether each artifact's generated files/globs exist — no separate state DB, the folder contents ARE the state.

Delta-spec discipline: changes never mutate the canonical openspec/specs/ tree; they write ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED/RENAMED delta files (MODIFIED must copy the whole requirement block), reconciled into source-of-truth only on archive.
One workflow definition fans out to 27 AI-tool adapters, each writing the same /opsx commands in that harness's native command format, with auto-detection of which tools the repo uses.

Standards are MINED from your existing codebase, not authored top-down: discover-standards reads 5-10 representative files, surfaces 'unusual / opinionated / tribal / consistent' patterns, asks 'why' for each, and only then writes a standard — turning implicit team conventions into versioned, injectable context.

Index-as-context-router: a hand-light standards/index.yml (folder/file/one-line-description) is the retrieval layer instead of a vector DB — inject-standards reads the cheap index, then lazily loads only the 2-5 full standard bodies that match the current work.

Spec folders are self-contained handoff units with a hard 'save docs first' invariant: Task 1 of every shape-spec plan is always 'Save spec documentation' (plan/shape/standards/references/visuals) before any code, making planning durable and discoverable.

Deliberate de-scoping in v3: the project EXPLICITLY retired its own orchestration phases, role system, and verifier subagents on the thesis that frontier models + Claude Code Plan mode now handle implementation/orchestration natively — so it focuses only on what tools still do badly: codebase-specific standards + spec quality.

Token-frugality is a stated authoring rule ('every word costs tokens'): standards must lead with the rule, use code over prose, skip the obvious — context-window economics baked into the writing guidance.

loop-engineering as a CITABLE shared skill: a single reference file gives every iterative skill the same 6-step loop, layered termination (deterministic verifier + iteration cap + budget guard + no-progress + human escalation), and a named-pattern vocabulary (ReAct/Reflexion/Evaluator-optimizer/Loop-until-dry) — DRY for agent control flow, with the dogma 'trust deterministic checks, never the agent's looks-done self-report.'

Tiered-enrichment people CRM gated purely by cross-source mention count (stub@1 / moderate@3+ / full@8+ or direct-meeting), append-only Timeline, every observation source-cited with [[wikilink]]+date+confidence, and an explicit 'don't force a full profile on first encounter' rule — anti-speculation by construction.

Worker Output Rule as a hard context firewall: a token-threshold table (<2K inline / >=2K to /tmp/{slug}.md) plus 'return only status+path' means heavy Sonnet output never pollutes the Opus reasoning window; the lead reads files, not transcripts.

Self-rewriting Zettelkasten KB where frameworks have a lifecycle (Emerging->Working->Stable), citations are MECHANICAL gates (any principle without >=1 source link is dropped), and monthly consolidation flips status captured->consolidated, dedups against existing frameworks, and archives superseded insights with a replacement note.

One skill/agent surface emitted across 4 runtimes (Claude 19, Kiro 7, Gemini 7, Codex via AGENTS.md) and policed by a CI validator that enforces count parity, AGENTS.md documentation coverage, and version alignment across plugin.json/marketplace/COG-VERSION.

Self-wiring knowledge graph: typed edges (works_at/invested_in/attended/...) are extracted on every put_page with ZERO LLM calls via a deterministic auto-link post-hook, with full provenance (link_source, origin_page_id) on each edge — and the README claims +31.4 P@5 over a graph-disabled variant.

The autonomous nightly 'dream cycle' (lint->backlinks->sync->synthesize->extract->patterns->embed->orphans): a cheap Haiku verdict filters routine sessions, Sonnet subagents fan out per transcript to write reflections/originals, and a separate patterns pass emits theme pages only when >=3 reflections support a pattern — all idempotent (content-hash keyed), privacy-filtered, and spend-capped by a cooldown.

skill-optimizer treats SKILL.md as the trainable parameters of a frozen agent: benchmark-graded text-space optimization with a MANDATORY median-of-3+epsilon validation gate, body-only edits, atomic versioned snapshots, and bundled-skill quarantine requiring a held-out set or it hard-refuses.

Skillpack-as-scaffolding: after install, gbrain stops being authoritative and becomes a diffable reference library — `gbrain skillpack reference <slug>` shows identical/differs/missing per skill and a two-way merge with a loud warning, so intentional local edits survive upgrades.

Routing moved from a managed-table block to pure frontmatter triggers discovered by walking skills/*/SKILL.md at runtime (substring + optional embedding match), with per-skill routing-eval.jsonl fixtures testing dispatch accuracy — routing is data, not a hand-maintained table.

Filesystem-convention document handoff with zero explicit path-passing: every skill drops a timestamped artifact in ~/.gstack/projects/$SLUG/ and the next skill rediscovers the latest by glob+timestamp — the baton is the folder, not a parameter.

Tiered preamble injection engine: a TS resolver composes a CUMULATIVE preamble (tier1 subset of tier2 subset of tier3) per skill's declared preamble-tier, with order treated as load-bearing (a code comment ties pacing-rule order to a real regression bug).

review-army parallel fan-out: scope-conditional selection of up to 7+ specialist reviewers launched in a single multi-Agent message, deduped by {path}:{line}:{category} fingerprint with multi-specialist agreement raising confidence and a confidence floor suppressing noise.
Decaying taste profiles computed at READ time (5%/week) that bias future generation and actively FLAG when the current request contradicts a strong stored signal.

Dual-voice cross-model verification (fresh-context Claude subagent + Codex with cross-phase memory) producing an explicit CONFIRMED/DISAGREE consensus table, with disagreements escalated to a human gate rather than silently resolved.

CONTEXT.md as a machine-greppable predicate registry (NAMESPACE.key=value lines) with a META.RULE forcing agent briefs to cite predicate IDs verbatim — turning a context doc into an anti-drift contract that grep and CI can both enforce.

Four-level artifact verification hierarchy (Exists → Substantive → Wired → Data-Flows) run by an adversarial 'falsify-first' fresh-context verifier — explicitly defeats the 'file exists so it's done' / stub-renders-empty-array failure mode.

Document-handoff chain RESEARCH→PLAN→SUMMARY→VERIFICATION where subagents return only a tiny status block ('## RESEARCH COMPLETE' + confidence + path) and the heavy artifact lives on disk — clean context firewall plus durable spec-as-baton.

Context-budget hook that injects warnings into the AGENT's own stream at 35%/25% remaining and drops a recovery breadcrumb subprocess before exhaustion — making the model, not just the human, context-aware.
Provenance-tagged research with a 'slopcheck' package-legitimacy audit: any dependency found outside official docs is forced to [ASSUMED] and routed to human validation before decisions lock.

Agents coordinate through a shared 'swarm/...' memory namespace (a blackboard) rather than message passing — each agent writes swarm/<role>/status and swarm/shared/<topic> and reads peers' keys before acting, making the durable memory store the coordination substrate.

A full hook lifecycle turns Claude Code's session events into a persistence engine: SessionStart imports+vectorizes memory, Stop/SessionEnd sync insights back and regenerate a graph-ordered MEMORY.md index, PreCompact restores-then-persists around context compaction, SubagentStop folds subagent results into memory (context firewall).

Consensus is implemented as actual agents: a Byzantine PBFT coordinator (f<n/3 fault tolerance), weighted voting where the Queen's vote counts 3x, and majority modes — agreement protocols promoted to first-class roles.

Dual-backend memory: a JSON Map store for fast CRUD plus an AgentDB SQLite+HNSW/ONNX-384 vector index for semantic recall, with keys structured as claude-memory:{project}:{file}:{section} and a cross-project 'claude-memories' namespace.

skill-builder meta-skill codifies a strict 3-level progressive-disclosure token budget (L1 ~200ch metadata always, L2 SKILL.md on trigger, L3 files on demand) as the explicit reason 100+ skills can coexist cheaply.

Rewrite-not-append success metric: ingest is judged a failure if it only adds new pages — 'If an ingest only creates new pages and doesn't rewrite anything, it wasn't deep enough' — forcing the LLM to integrate into existing biographies/concepts rather than pile up notes.

Bi-temporal facts as an append-only `timeline:` frontmatter array capturing both when a fact was true and when the vault learned it, enabling 'you believed X Tuesday, shifted to Z after Wednesday's ingest' reflective queries.

Progressive L0-L3 token-budgeted context engine (~170 tok identity -> ~120-tok CRITICAL_FACTS core -> index catalog -> daily/board state -> on-demand deep context) that lets a fresh session resume work without loading the whole vault.

'For future Claude' machine-first preamble on every note + an executable lint hook (validate-ai-first.sh) that actually greps for the preamble, ai-first:true frontmatter, and banned unicode — the AI-first contract is enforced, not aspirational.
Propagation matrix / two-output rule: every answer also writes back, and every write must cascade to 3-5 related notes (board, daily note, index) so the graph never accumulates orphans.
One source tree compiles to six CLI runtimes via adapters/ + build.sh, each emitting a platform-native dispatcher (CLAUDE.md / GEMINI.md / AGENTS.md / .agents/skills / .pi / Hermes).

Lifecycle-hook memory persistence: SessionStart injects the markdown vault, while PreCompact AND SessionEnd both spawn a zero-tool background flush agent that distills the conversation into the daily log — with an os.environ['CLAUDE_INVOKED_BY'] recursion guard so a flush can't trigger another flush.

Two-phase cost-optimized proactivity: Python gathers all integration data deterministically and only THEN invokes Claude to reason, with snapshot state-diffing so notifications fire only on change — quantified at ~$0.05/run vs ~$0.38 if Claude made live MCP tool calls.

Python CLI wrapper isolation (query.py gmail list): every integration is a Python module with auth + query fns + a CLI subcommand, so Claude only ever receives structured data and never sees API keys, hardened by a block-secrets.py PreToolUse hook that blocks env-dumping commands and secret-file reads.
Self-deleting BOOTSTRAP.md onboarding wizard that interviews one question at a time, writes USER.md/SOUL.md/HEARTBEAT.md, then rm's itself so it never re-runs.

Markdown-as-source-of-truth with a layered SQLite hybrid index: canonical memory is human-readable markdown; FastEmbed/sqlite-vec + FTS5 (0.7/0.3 fusion) is just a derived retrieval layer, incrementally re-indexed only on changed files.

5-tier Ebbinghaus memory: tier (core/active/warm/cold/archive) is a frontmatter field; relevance decays as 1 - rate*days_since_access with per-type rates, access_count = 1+ln(n) slows decay, and a 'touch' promotes a card exactly ONE tier — a real spacing-effect forgetting curve implemented in markdown + a Python graph engine.

Subscription-not-API economics as an architecture: instead of per-message API calls, the system drives ONE long-lived interactive Claude Code session in tmux for the main loop, and even the parallel haiku->sonnet bootstrap swarm runs 'without API keys' through that session — explicitly replacing the deprecated claude -p pipeline to avoid Agent SDK billing.

Hybrid deterministic-script + LLM-judgment graph maintenance: graph.py computes a 0-100 health score (broken links, orphans, description coverage) deterministically and gates actions on thresholds (>=90 good, fix --apply when broken>0), while the Claude agent owns the un-scriptable parts (classification, dedup, link suggestion).

Isolated cron-session as a context firewall: scheduled prompts run in a fresh session with zero access to live conversation, forcing fully self-contained prompts — clean separation of the always-on interactive brain from autonomous scheduled work.

Orphan-prevention as a hard CREATE invariant: a new card is only valid if it links to its domain hub plus >=2 siblings on creation, and dedup is mandated BEFORE link enrichment so the graph never links to soon-merged files.
SQLite FTS5 (Porter-stemmed memory_fts virtual table) + regex entity extraction over a flat markdown brain/ — a real DB-backed memory layer with zero vector infra, reindexed via an HTTP endpoint.

APScheduler cron engine whose jobs are individual JSON files in scheduler/jobs/, hot-reloaded by a 2-second-poll file-watcher (JobFileWatcher diffs mtimes -> fires _reload_jobs callback) so cron changes take effect without restarting the server.

Self-improving skill folder = SKILL.md + eval.json (weighted scoring criteria) + learnings.md + context/, with a weekly memory-consolidation skill that prunes each skill's score-history to the latest 20 and dedups learnings — a closed quality/learning loop.
Every skill declares Primary+Fallback runtime among three free-tier agents, so skill->agent dispatch and graceful degradation are encoded at the skill level and enforced by a constitution principle.

constitution.md (6 principles + 3 prohibitions + mandatory 7-step pre-task workflow) operationalized programmatically — Cost Consciousness becomes the cost-analytics 80/90/95% alert skill, Transparency becomes the audit log + git auto-commit.

Strict context firewall by construction: ONE subagent (source_writer) is the only thing in the whole system that ever reads a raw source; everything downstream reads its synthesized Layer-1.5 page, so the heavy raw content is structurally invisible to the orchestrator and all peers.

INDEX-based progressive drilling with zero vector DB — a query escalates index.yaml summary → wiki source page → user highlights → raw, stopping the moment the answer is reached, getting RAG-like selectivity purely from markdown/index traversal.

Determinism where it matters: synthesis is delegated to LLM subagents, but index building (build_index_yaml.py / build_index_md.py), dedup (dedup_findings.py), and timestamp/merge handling are deterministic Python — byte-stable, idempotent, --existing-index merge — so re-runs don't drift.

Self-maintaining KB: research-lint runs mechanical graph checks (orphans, missing hubs at >=3 mentions, missing comparisons at >=2 mutual links, broken wikilinks) plus LLM checks (stale/contradictory claims) and auto-appends contradictions + open-questions — the wiki audits and reconciles itself.

Round-based discovery with an explicit gap_analyzer between waves that grades theme coverage (well-covered/thin/missing) from metadata only and emits the next wave's queries — a planner→worker loop that narrows from broad themes to specific tools/authors over rounds.

Executable PM skill done right: rice_prioritizer.py is a real stdlib-only argparse CLI (CSV/'sample' in; text/json/csv out) that computes RICE and auto-buckets quick-wins vs big-bets by an impact×effort matrix — a deterministic, no-LLM tool the agent shells out to, packaged inside the skill folder.

Session-handoff as a hook-driven plugin: a 5-section handoff doc (Goal/State/Decisions/Skills-to-use/Artifacts) auto-injected at SessionStart via session_start.py as <handoff_from_previous_session> context and nagged for at SessionEnd — with a hard 'reference artifacts by path/commit, never duplicate' discipline and a redaction linter before save.

Self-improvement with a promotion ladder: patterns auto-captured to a transient MEMORY.md, then graduated only after recurring 2-3x via /si:review→human-approve→/si:promote into permanent CLAUDE.md/rules, with the MEMORY.md entry deleted to free budget — explicit transient-vs-permanent memory tiering plus a skill-extractor quarantine.

A binding skill-authoring contract enforced by CI: every SKILL.md must carry fixed sections (Before Starting context-checkpoint, Proactive Triggers, Output-Artifacts table, Related-Skills WHEN/NOT, confidence-tagging) and is run through audit_skills.py / derive_counters.py / check_plugin_json.py for PASS/WARN/FAIL — skills-as-product-with-QA.

Path-B megaprompt→skill factory: a raw expert megaprompt becomes SKILL.md verbatim, then a wrapper auto-generates the validators, 5-7-source references, a cs-* persona agent, a /cs:* command, and provenance metadata — a documented meta-pipeline that manufactures conforming skills at scale (345 of them).

The single most transferable idea: a uniform JSON 'context-request' template baked into every agent prompt (request_type: get_<domain>_context + a payload.query that spells out exactly which facts the role needs) — turns 'gather context' from a vague instruction into a typed, role-scoped pull request.

Extreme template rigidity at scale: 154+ agents across 10 categories all share the same 6-part anatomy (identity → invocation protocol → 8-item checklists → Communication Protocol JSON → 3-phase workflow → collaborator list), and almost every list is forced to exactly 8 items — a content-template that makes mass authoring and consistency-checking trivial.

Per-role model + tool tiering encoded in frontmatter: doc-generation roles (PM, scrum) get haiku and a no-Bash read/write/web allowlist; the more analytical business-analyst gets sonnet — model cost and least-privilege tools are a property of each persona file.
Each agent ships its own collaborator graph (8 named sibling agents) so the catalog self-describes hand-off paths without a central orchestrator — a decentralized router-by-convention.
Distribution as per-category plugins (claude plugin marketplace add … then install voltagent-<cat>) plus an agent-installer subagent that installs peers on demand.

Author-once-in-markdown, generate-native-everywhere: one plugins/<name>/ source compiles via tools/adapters/*.py into 6 harness-native artifact sets, with each adapter handling model-alias mapping, body-size caps (Codex 8KB→references/), and permission/case normalization — DRY agent authoring across the whole ecosystem.

plugin-eval three-layer quality framework: static (instant, layers/static.py) + LLM-judge over 4 semantic dimensions (layers/judge.py, ~30s/4 calls) + Monte-Carlo of ~50 real invocations (layers/monte_carlo.py) blended per-dimension with layer-specific weights (e.g. triggering_accuracy weights MC 0.60/judge 0.25/static 0.15; progressive_disclosure weights static 0.80) into a 10-dimension composite → A+–F grade + Platinum–Bronze badge.

Real statistical rigor in stats.py: Wilson-score and Clopper-Pearson exact CIs, bootstrap percentile CI, coefficient-of-variation for consistency, and Cohen's kappa for inter-judge agreement — quality scoring treated as measurement, not vibes.

Elo-rated skill corpus: skills carry persisted Elo ratings (corpus.py + elo.py); evaluation selects nearest-rated same-category skills as reference anchors, so quality is graded relatively against the existing catalog.

Anti-pattern penalties applied multiplicatively to the composite (engine._assemble_composite via anti_pattern_penalty) — over-constraining language, empty descriptions, missing 'use when' triggers, and dead cross-references mechanically dock the score.

A full Claude Code lifecycle-hook suite in one hooks.json: SessionStart context-loader, PostToolUse suggest-compact, PreCompact context-preserver, and SessionEnd learning-log that auto-commits session insights — persistence and a capture loop wired purely through harness events, no agent reasoning required.

A dedicated context-manager agent that turns token budgeting into an explicit numeric policy (40% modified files / 20% reference / skip vendor) and defines a context-firewall handoff briefing contract that names exactly what to exclude (debug history, rejected approaches, tangents).

multi-agent-coordinator codifies five reusable orchestration topologies (Pipeline, Map-Reduce, Supervisor, Debate-with-judge, Iterative-Refinement) as a vocabulary over dynamically-assigned functional roles rather than hardwired agent names.

Skills-as-package-manager: 35 vendored SKILL.md folders (frontmatter preserves original author/repo) sitting atop a 'npx skills add owner/repo' CLI into a 400k-skill external marketplace — curation layer over a registry.

PM capability is delivered as standalone role-persona files (product-manager, scrum-master, project-manager) with methodology baked in (RICE, JTBD, Given/When/Then) and hard output contracts (≥3 acceptance criteria/story, rollback criteria) — no PM skill, no PM command.

One shared .agents/product-marketing.md context file is the keystone: a single foundation skill writes 12 sections of positioning once, and all 44 other skills open by reading it before asking anything — eliminating context re-entry across an entire marketing workflow.

CLAUDE.md is literally one line ('AGENTS.md') pointing at the cross-agent doc, and AGENTS.md quarantines Claude-Code-only syntax (the !`command` context-injection trick) OUT of SKILL.md files to keep skills portable across Codex/Cursor/Windsurf.

Two-tier validation: a self-contained bash linter (awk frontmatter extraction + regex name/description/length/trigger-word/cross-ref checks) plus the official agentskills skills-ref validator, wired into a CI build-matrix that fans changed SKILL.md dirs out for per-skill validation.

A sync-skills CI job auto-derives marketplace.json, plugin.json, and the README skill table from the skills/ folder on every push and self-commits — the catalog can never drift from the actual skills.
51 zero-dependency Node CLIs with a universal --dry-run that echoes the redacted request without sending, giving skills a safe executable layer over GA4/Stripe/Semrush/HubSpot etc.

Pure-parallel fan-out across 5 fixed analytic DIMENSIONS (not tasks): one agent per scoring axis, each with its own page-fetch + per-band 0-10 rubric, all launched simultaneously over the same shared discovery payload, then reduced by a weighted-composite formula into a single 0-100 score — a clean map-reduce where the reduce weights are the product's opinion (Content 25% > Conversion/SEO 20% > Competitive 15% > Brand/Growth 10%).

Context firewall by output contract: every subagent's prompt ends with a rigid return-block template (score table + Top Wins + Critical Fixes + Before/After + Missing), so only the compact structured summary flows back to the orchestrator while each agent's raw WebFetch dumps stay in its own context.

Discovery-before-dispatch: a Phase-1 business-type classifier (6 types via detection-signal table) + site page-map is computed once and injected into all 5 agents so the parallel work is steered by shared business framing rather than each agent re-deriving it.

File-existence as the coordination bus: skills read/write named Markdown artifacts in cwd (COMPETITOR-REPORT.md, BRAND-VOICE.md, SEO-AUDIT.md...) and opportunistically fold in whichever exist — a zero-infra doc-handoff chain, culminating in market-report-pdf vacuuming up 9 known filenames into one JSON for the reportlab PDF.

LLM-produces-JSON, code-produces-pixels: the PDF skill restricts the model to emitting an exact JSON schema, then a bundled reportlab script deterministically renders gauges/bar-charts/tables with a fixed brand palette — clean separation of judgment from rendering for client-ready deliverables.

Whole-domain decomposition: one /seo entry skill routes (via a 28-row command table) to 25 narrow sub-skills, which in turn delegate to 18 tool-scoped specialist agents and ~50 deterministic Python scripts — a clean directive/orchestration/execution 3-layer split.

Conditional dependency-gated fan-out: the audit orchestrator spawns 8 specialists always but gates 7 more on business-type detection, MCP/credential availability, and existence of a prior drift baseline — parallelism keyed on runtime signals, not a fixed roster.

Structured artifact handoff: analysis writes a typed audit-data.json envelope that a separate google_report.py step consumes to render a charted A4 PDF — the JSON is the baton between agent reasoning and deterministic report rendering.

SQLite-backed drift memory: baselines.db stores SHA-256-hashed page snapshots with URL-normalization and a 17-rule comparison engine, giving the agent cross-session memory of a site's SEO state to detect regressions.

Portability as a first-class design goal: identical skill/agent markdown is auto-discovered by Claude Code, Cursor, Antigravity and Gemini CLI via plugin.json + AGENTS.md, and delegation degrades to inline sequential execution when a harness lacks subagents.

A domain-transfer of Spec-Kit: the entire spec→plan→tasks→implement scaffold is kept verbatim but the 'tests' and 'success criteria' slots are re-bound to growth metrics (k-factor, time-to-wow, NRR) and the constitution to growth principles — showing how a coding-agent OS can be re-skinned to a non-code function without changing the orchestration machinery.

Constitution-as-gate: 7 principles compiled into explicit Mandatory vs Advisory 'Constitution Check Gates' that the plan command must pass before Phase-0 research and that BLOCK implementation unless a violation is justified in a Complexity Tracking section — anti-drift governance with a fail-closed default.

TDD-for-growth: Principle VI reframes Red-Green-Refactor as a mandatory pilot phase (<10% budget) with a go/no-go validation checkpoint where /growthkit.implement literally PAUSES and offers Halt/Pivot/Proceed if k-factor<0.8 or engagement<5%.

Self-documenting amendment trail: the constitution carries an embedded SYNC IMPACT REPORT header (version delta, which principles changed, which templates need updating) plus a semver amendment process — the governance doc tracks its own evolution machine-readably.

The spec/plan/tasks artifacts ARE the context store: no vector DB, no memory layer — durable state lives entirely in numbered git-branched campaign folders with a JSON-emitting bootstrap script as the hand-off contract between phases.

The three-level loading contract (metadata always-on ~100 words / SKILL.md body on-trigger <500 lines / bundled scripts that execute WITHOUT being read into context) is stated as an explicit budget — the canonical formulation of progressive disclosure that every PM-OS repo inherits.

skill-creator is a self-improving meta-skill: it builds a skill, then runs paired with-skill vs baseline subagent A/B evals, a grader that rejects surface-compliance, and a held-out-test description optimizer that picks the best trigger phrasing by generalization rather than train score.

Description-as-the-only-trigger discipline: 'all when-to-use info goes in the description, not the body', plus deliberately 'pushy', keyword-dense, exclusion-bearing descriptions (docx's 'Do NOT use for PDFs...') to fight under-triggering — with a measurable optimizer to tune it.

Subagents shipped as plain markdown Role/Inputs/Process/Output-Format docs inside the skill's own agents/ folder, JIT-loaded only when spawned — a lightweight, registry-free agent-definition convention.

Authoring philosophy baked into skill-creator: 'explain the WHY instead of ALL-CAPS MUSTs', generalize from few examples (avoid overfit), and 'if 3 test runs all wrote the same helper script, bundle that script' — guidance that directly shapes how good skills get written.

code-review encodes a 5-lens parallel reviewer panel + per-issue Haiku confidence scoring (verbatim 0-100 rubric, filter <80) + cheap-model eligibility triage entirely inside ONE command markdown file — no agent files, pure prompt orchestration with explicit model-tiering.

skill-creator is a meta-skill that builds, eval-tests (background runs + generate_review.py), and then runs a description-improver to make a new skill's trigger description deliberately 'pushy' against under-triggering — skills treated as benchmarked, optimized artifacts.

The marketplace is a pure pointer index: 243 plugins as sha-pinned git references (url / git-subdir / local) resolved lazily on install, with a strict:false skills[] mode that curates arbitrary skill subsets out of third-party SDK repos that never adopted the plugin manifest.

Agent definitions are read-only by tool allowlist (code-explorer gets Grep/Read/etc. but no Edit/Write) and pinned to a model, then fanned out 2-3 in parallel on different facets, returning file lists the orchestrator reads — a clean context-firewall + least-privilege pattern.

example-plugin documents that commands/*.md and skills/<name>/SKILL.md load through the identical engine — the only difference is file layout — collapsing the commands-vs-skills distinction to where the file sits, with skills/ as the forward format.
